artifacts.content:
Laura,

Kids are finally in bed and I’m sitting here staring at tomorrow’s route, trying not to spiral. Today at Willow Glen Court is going to stick with me for a long time. I keep replaying that moment around 10:35 a.m. when I was standing by the truck, rereading your text about Emily’s panic attack at Amelia Earhart and Ms. Rodriguez saying she might need to see someone at Riverside Mental Health Clinic. I glanced down at my phone instead of watching Jason finish with the hitch. A few minutes later the chipper was vibrating, the driveway at 7821 sloping just enough, and then that horrible slow roll before the trailer slammed into Jonathan Reed’s Camry.

I shut everything down, checked on Daniel after he tried to grab the rail and tweaked his wrist, and then had to walk over and face Jonathan. He was actually calmer than I expected, just understandably upset about his 2021 Toyota getting its front bumper and headlight smashed in. I told him I’d take responsibility and that I’d already tightened up our safety stuff, but inside I just felt like I’d failed everyone.

On top of that, I still had Dad’s 9:00 a.m. call in my head about Mom’s dementia getting worse and the neurologist at Riverside Community Hospital pushing for memory care in Moreno Valley within 60 days. I keep thinking about that $18,000 line of credit and wondering how I’m supposed to cover family stuff and business mistakes.

Anyway, thanks for handling the school chaos with Emily and Jacob while I was stuck dealing with this mess. Let’s talk tomorrow night about Emily’s counselor options and maybe carving out a real family day this weekend.

– Mike

metadata.subject: Long day – Willow Glen and Emily

artifacts.content:
Hey Sarah,

I figured I’d write instead of texting a wall of messages. It’s been a rough stretch.

You already know about the chipper trailer rolling into that silver 2021 Camry on Willow Glen Court. What I didn’t really say is how much of it was on me. I was wiped out that morning from my back flaring up again – same old 2019 injury from when I first did PT at Riverside Orthopedic & Spine Center. I barely slept, took one of the leftover hydrocodone Dr. Patel prescribed back then around 6:30 a.m., and I was definitely groggy. I let Jason handle moving the truck and never double‑checked the hitch before we fired up the chipper. A few hours later, there goes the trailer into Jonathan Reed’s car.

Then Dad called at 9:00 a.m. from Moreno Valley, saying Mom’s early‑stage dementia has gotten worse and her neurologist at Riverside Community Hospital wants her in a memory care place within the next 60 days. I was standing in the driveway at 7821 Willow Glen, thinking about touring facilities and how I’m going to pay for any of this when I just opened that $18,000 line of credit for the business.

Meanwhile, Daniel’s wrist sprain from trying to stop the trailer means more light duty. I took him over to Riverside Urgent Care on Arlington; Dr. Patel there said it’s “just” a sprain, but I’m trying to keep him off heavy lifting for a bit.

All this while Emily’s having panic attacks at Amelia Earhart and Ms. Rodriguez is talking about a referral to Riverside Mental Health Clinic. I feel like I’m dropping balls everywhere – kids, parents, crew.

Any nurse‑big‑sister words of wisdom are welcome. Also, if you know any decent memory care options in Moreno Valley that aren’t insanely expensive, send them my way.

Love,
Mike

metadata.subject: Mom, my back, and that trailer mess

artifacts.content:
Personal journal – 2025-11-18

Need to get my head straight about everything swirling around this trailer accident and the people involved.

Daniel R – full-time tech since June 2021. Good worker, and honestly more tangled up in our life than most employees. He helped watch Emily and Jacob back in Feb–Mar 2024 when Laura was staying with her mom in Moreno Valley during our trial separation, even drove Emily to Amelia Earhart and Jacob to Washington Elementary in the work truck when I had early commercial jobs. I also send him over to Dad’s condo in Moreno Valley some Saturdays since Dad’s mild cognitive impairment diagnosis at Riverside Medical Center in June 2023 – slip him $80–$100 cash to do yardwork and keep an eye on Dad.

Health-wise, Daniel’s wrist sprain from grabbing the trailer rail is still on my mind. When we were at Riverside Urgent Care on Arlington on 1/18/25, the triage nurse read off his blood pressure – 168/102 – and asked if he was still taking that lisinopril from Kaiser in Moreno Valley. He admitted he’d stopped. Later he quietly told the PA he’s been taking leftover hydrocodone from a 2023 dental surgery at Riverside Dental Center and sometimes washing it down with 3–4 beers after work. I’m worried about him as a person, not just as an employee.

He also mentioned to Dr. Patel that he’d had a similar fall at a Corona job in Oct 2023 but never said anything because he was scared it might mess up his adjustment-of-status case with Martinez & Flores Law Group.

Need to find a way to support him without overstepping.

metadata.title: Journal – Daniel, family, and health stuff

artifacts.content:
Dad,

Just wanted to catch you up after the other day. I know when I called you sounded worn out, and I don’t want you worrying more than you already are.

I’ve been sending Daniel over to your condo on a lot of Saturdays since your mild cognitive impairment diagnosis at Riverside Medical Center back in June 2023. He does the yard, checks the lights and outlets, that kind of stuff. I usually give him about $80–$100 cash for the day. He doesn’t mind and actually likes getting out of Riverside for a bit.

He’s the same Daniel who hurt his wrist when the chipper trailer rolled into that Camry on Willow Glen. When I took him to Riverside Orthopedic & Sports Medicine on February 10, 2025, Dr. Nguyen said if the wrist doesn’t keep improving they might need an MRI and maybe a small outpatient procedure. That could cut into his hours with me and maybe even qualify him for partial disability. I’m trying to be fair about light duty and not push him.

On my side, I’m still kicking myself about that runaway trailer. Between that and the old 2022 small‑claims mess in Temecula – the $7,800 settlement over the cracked stamped‑concrete driveway – I feel like every mistake turns into a big bill. That’s part of why I finally opened that $18,000 business line of credit.

How are you feeling about the neurologist at Riverside Community Hospital talking memory care in Moreno Valley for Mom? Have you two talked more about it? I can drive out this Sunday after Jacob’s soccer and we can look at some options online together.

Love,
Mike

metadata.subject: Checking in on you and Mom

artifacts.content:
Event: Daniel – medical + work follow-ups
Date: 2025-02-10
Time: 3:30 p.m. – 5:00 p.m.
Location: Riverside Orthopedic & Sports Medicine; then shop yard
Attendees: Michael Thompson, Daniel Ruiz

Notes:
– 3:30 p.m. appointment with Dr. Nguyen at Riverside Orthopedic & Sports Medicine (follow-up on Daniel’s right wrist after January incident). Ask about:
  • Whether he still thinks it’s just a sprain or if we’re heading toward MRI / minor outpatient procedure.
  • How long Daniel should stay on light duty at Thompson Garden & Landscaping Services.
  • Any risk this limits him from full-time field work long term or pushes him toward partial disability.

– Remember Daniel’s other health stuff from earlier urgent care visit on Arlington: BP 168/102, lapsed lisinopril from Kaiser Moreno Valley, and the PA’s concern about him using leftover hydrocodone from 2023 dental surgery at Riverside Dental Center plus 3–4 beers after work. Not my business to bring it up in detail, but keep an ear out for whether he’s taking care of himself.

– After appointment, swing by Dad’s condo in Moreno Valley to drop Daniel off for a couple of hours of yardwork and minor fixes (set aside $80 cash). Good chance for him to check on Dad since the mild cognitive impairment diagnosis.

– Pick up Jacob from AYSO practice on the way back if timing works.

metadata.title: Daniel – ortho follow-up + Dad check-in
metadata.location: Riverside Orthopedic & Sports Medicine / Moreno Valley

artifacts.content:
Laura,

Quick update while I’m eating a late sandwich at the counter.

Jonathan Reed swung by the house on the 18th with his wife Melissa and their little boy to look at how I had the trailer and chipper set up. We walked out to the driveway at 2147 and I showed him the heavier wheel chocks and secondary safety chain I added after the accident on Willow Glen. While we were standing there, Melissa mentioned their son was diagnosed with Type 1 diabetes at Riverside Community Hospital in September and they still owe about $3,000. No wonder Jonathan’s so stressed about the $4,850 estimate and rental car.

He’s been emailing a lot. On the 16th he forwarded something he’d sent to his HR manager, Karen Lopez, at Riverside County Public Health, saying this whole thing is triggering his anxiety and panic attacks and that he’s been seeing Dr. Alan Pierce at Riverside Behavioral Health Clinic since around March 2023. Then on the 17th he texted that he’s a payment behind on his Camry loan with Inland Empire Credit Union – about $420 – and is scared they’ll repossess the car if things drag out. Last night he sent another long message and CC’d his cousin, attorney Mark Delgado at Delgado & Ruiz downtown, hinting at small claims and “negligent business practices.”

I get that they’re under pressure, especially with their kid’s health, but it’s a lot to carry on top of Emily’s panic attack at Amelia Earhart and Ms. Rodriguez talking about Riverside Mental Health Clinic, plus all the stuff with Mom and Dad.

Let’s sit down after dinner tomorrow and look at our own budget again. I want to make sure we’re ready for slower winter months even if this drags on.

– M

Job + life log – early Feb 2026

Feb 4 – Sprayed pesticide at Washington Elementary (Jacob’s school) for gophers and weeds around the back fields. Made sure to do it after dismissal and followed all label rules, but still felt weird working where he goes to 4th grade.

Feb 6 – Willow Glen Court again, across from Jonathan Reed’s place. While repositioning the truck and chipper trailer near 7818, I was on the phone with Mom about Dad’s cardiology appointment at Riverside Community Hospital later that afternoon. Got distracted, didn’t set the brake as firmly as I should have, and the trailer rolled just enough to bump a Camry parked along the curb – cracked the front bumper on the driver’s side, shattered the left headlight, dented the fender. When I checked the trailer frame I noticed dried pesticide residue from the Washington Elementary job had streaked onto his paint near the wheel arch.

It wasn’t nearly as bad as the November 2025 runaway trailer into Jonathan’s 2021 Camry, but still another reminder how fast things go sideways when my head’s half on family health stuff and half on work. Dad’s heart, Mom’s dementia, Emily’s anxiety at Amelia Earhart, Jacob’s soccer and homework, Daniel’s wrist – it all piles up.

Need to:
– Add “no phone while moving truck/trailer” to the morning safety huddle.
– Double-check trailer brakes and chocks every stop.
– Keep a better log of where we’ve applied pesticides, especially at schools.
– Schedule one quiet night a week with no work talk so Laura and I can actually breathe.

metadata.title: Job log – Willow Glen + school spray

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <m.thompsonlandscape@gmail.com>
To: Laura Thompson <laura.thompson2147@gmail.com>
Date: February 7, 2026
Subject: Notes for insurance & lawyer about the trailer incident

Laura,

I’m trying to get all this straight in my head before I talk to the Southsure adjuster and then to Alvarez (the lawyer) about what happened on Willow Glen Court.

First, the car: when the trailer‑mounted chipper rolled and hit Jonathan Reed’s 2021 Camry around 10:30 a.m. on February 6, 2026 in front of 7821 Willow Glen Court, it nailed the front driver‑side corner. The impact cracked the front bumper cover, shattered the left headlight lens, and put a deep crease in the front left fender. I took a bunch of photos from different angles, but I had to edit a couple because the magnet on the side of my truck was visible. It’s the one about the discounted lawn‑care program I set up last fall for low‑income seniors at the Moreno Valley mobile home park where Mom and Dad (Bob and Elaine) have their lot. The magnet lists my direct cell and the special pricing, so I don’t want that floating around in the claim file.

Weird twist: I’m almost positive Jonathan Reed is the same guy from my DUI diversion group at Riverside Counseling Center that I had to do after the April 2022 arrest. I was there from May through August 2022. We never talked, but I remember him saying in one session he was on probation and worried about keeping his job at the auto body shop on Magnolia Avenue.

On top of that, he’s also the guy who left that note on my truck at the Stater Bros. parking lot on Arlington back in November 2023, complaining our trailer blocked two spaces and threatening to “take this to small claims court” if it happened again. I still have the photo of the note on my phone from November 18, 2023.

Just wanted you to know how all these pieces connect.

– Michael

metadata.subject: Notes for insurance & lawyer about the trailer incident

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <m.thompsonlandscape@gmail.com>
To: Sarah Martinez <smartinezRN@gmail.com>
Date: February 8, 2026
Subject: Need your take on Daniel’s wrist situation

Hey Sarah,

I could use your nurse brain. You know Daniel Ruiz, my full‑time landscape tech who’s been on the payroll since June 2021. He’s also been living in our converted garage here at 2147 Oakridge Drive since March 2023, after that domestic violence mess with his ex‑girlfriend. The restraining order went through at Riverside County Superior Court on March 14, 2023, and I told him he could stay rent‑free if he helped with emergency jobs and drove when my anxiety spikes.

He was the one who tweaked his right wrist during the runaway trailer incident. When I was filling out the injury section for the paperwork, I almost wrote down everything he told Dr. Patel at Riverside Urgent Care on Arlington Avenue on February 3, 2025. Daniel mentioned the wrist sprain might aggravate an old carpal tunnel issue from a second warehouse job he had back in 2022. On top of that, he’s already on a modified‑duty note from his primary care at Riverside Medical Clinic, limiting repetitive motions for the next 6 months.

I’m trying to balance being honest without oversharing his medical history in the report. Practically, I’ve kept him on light tasks—no heavy lifting, no long stretches on the trimmer—especially because I still lean on him when my freeway anxiety flares up from that panic attack I had driving the work truck on the 91 last October.

From your perspective, what’s reasonable to put in writing for the insurer versus what should stay between him and his doctors?

Love,
Michael

metadata.subject: Need your take on Daniel’s wrist situation

artifacts.content:
Title: Personal/therapy prep – work, accident, and relationships
Date: February 10, 2026
Author: Michael Thompson

Things to bring up with Dr. Klein:

1. Trailer accident + anxiety
- Go over the November 14, 2025 trailer scare on Willow Glen Court and how it’s still in my head.
- Connect it to the earlier panic attack driving the work truck on the State Route 91 freeway in Riverside in October 2025 – hands went numb, heart racing, had to pull off.
- Since then I’ve tightened safety (checklists, wheel chocks, extra safety chains) but I’m still replaying both that and the more recent February 6, 2026 incident with Jonathan Reed’s Camry.

2. Daniel’s situation at home and work
- Daniel Ruiz has been with Thompson Garden & Landscaping Services full‑time since June 2021.
- He moved into the converted garage at 2147 Oakridge Drive in March 2023 after the domestic violence incident with his ex‑girlfriend and the restraining order filed at Riverside County Superior Court on March 14, 2023.
- I let him stay rent‑free if he’s on‑call for emergency jobs and helps when my anxiety flares up (especially with freeway driving).
- He’s dealing with the right wrist sprain from the job, plus the old carpal tunnel from his 2022 warehouse second job, and the 6‑month modified‑duty note from Riverside Medical Clinic.

3. Guilt and boundaries
- Feeling responsible for Daniel’s injuries and housing.
- Worry I’m mixing boss/landlord/friend roles too much.
- Need to talk about how my DUI arrest in April 2022 and the Riverside Counseling Center diversion group (May–August 2022) still feed into this guilt and over‑accommodating people.
